title: Design a Dashboard UI
tagline: Design a high-fidelity analytics dashboard with data visualisation and filters.
scenario: A logistics company needs an operations dashboard showing delivery volumes, on-time rates, driver performance, and regional breakdowns. The ops manager checks it every morning.
brief: Design the dashboard specification. Choose the right chart types for each metric, design the filter system, and plan the responsive layout for desktop and tablet.
deliverables: A dashboard layout specification with at least 6 widgets: chart type, data it shows, and placement
A filter system design: which dimensions are filterable (date range, region, driver) and where filters live
A responsive strategy: how the dashboard adapts from desktop (1440px) to tablet (768px)
One data visualisation anti-pattern you avoided and why (e.g., "no pie charts for comparing 8 categories")
submissionGuidance: A dashboard is not a data dump. Every widget should answer a specific question. If you cannot state the question, the widget should not exist.

rubric.label: Widget Design
rubric.description: Are the chart types appropriate for the data they display?

rubric.label: Filter System
rubric.description: Is the filter system practical and non-destructive?

rubric.label: Responsive Strategy
rubric.description: Does the responsive adaptation preserve information hierarchy?

rubric.label: Anti-Pattern Awareness
rubric.description: Is the avoided anti-pattern genuine with sound reasoning?
